issue_title: UnicodeEncodeError in furl.join using Python 2.7
issue_body:
Loving furl, it's a great interface for URL-handling! Found this inconsistency in the API while using Python 2.7:

e.g.

```python
>>> furl(u'http://example.org/?kødpålæg=42')
furl('http://example.org/?k%C3%B8dp%C3%A5l%C3%A6g=42')
>>> furl('http://example.org').join(u'/?kødpålæg=42')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<input>", line 1, in <module>
    furl('http://example.org').join(u'/kødpålæg=42')
  File "/Users/jeffbr/.virtualenvs/clipper/lib/python2.7/site-packages/furl/furl.py", line 1260, in join
    self.load(urljoin(self.url, str(url)))
UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode character u'\xf8' in position 2: ordinal not in range(128)
```

Related to <https://github.com/gruns/furl/issues/33>, it looks like `urljoin` needs to handle 2.7's unicode strings, like `furl.__init__` does?

issue_title: dcc_listen with port selection
issue_body:
Hello,
I'm using your irc lib from a while now and it is awsome, thanks for your work and sharing it !

I would like to be able to select the port for dcc_listen connection, I think the modification needed are the following ones : 

In client.py

line 1046 :
class DCCConnection:
def listen(self): 
==> def listen(self, PORT=0):

line 1061:
self.socket.bind((socket.gethostbyname(socket.gethostname()), 0))
==> self.socket.bind((socket.gethostbyname(socket.gethostname()), PORT))  

line 1237:
def dcc_listen(self, dcctype="chat"):
==> def dcc_listen(self, dcctype="chat", PORT=0):

line 1244:
dcc.listen()
==> dcc.listen(PORT)

It would help me if you can integrate these modifications (which shouldn't have consequences for people using the actual version of irc module), so I can select port with dcc_listen(PORT) directly with module installed by "pip install irc"

Luc

The `process_content` method has `filename` and `content_type` args, but the `DepotFileInfo` call it with `process_content(content)`, because `DepotFileInfo` does not accept the other parameters.

I want to do this: `UploadedFile(content, filename="filename.txt")`

issue_title: send notifcation when in another terminal tab
issue_body:
when running with
```
export AUTO_NTFY_DONE_UNFOCUSED_ONLY=-b 
```

Notification are send only when the window isn't focused, but when one works with multiple tabs open, 
it will be nice to have notifications sent too. The solution right now is to completely disable this option
and accept that notifications will be sent even if the current tab is focused.

issue_title: Fail if a configured linter is not found
issue_body:
If a linter is listed in the configuration (eg: isort) pylama should fail if the linter is not found instead of giving only a warning: https://github.com/klen/pylama/blob/4e1994af951266467585b4fead84ecd5afca5c53/pylama/config.py#L68

This could be implemented as a setting to enforce or not enforce.

issue_title: Library doesn't support compressed XPORT files - Warning message
issue_body:
The library doesn't support compressed XPORT files.
I.e. files starting with 

       **COMPRESSED** **COMPRESSED** **COMPRESSED** **COMPRESSED** **COMPRESSED**". 

Example files can be fetched from https://www.ctti-clinicaltrials.org/aact-database.

Having support for these files would be great, but might be complicated to do, as the format specification is not available.

Can you add a error message, that the XPORT file is a compressed XPORT (CPORT) file, which is not supported by the library?

Thanks.

issue_body:
Tested with: 
* Fedora 26 64bits
* The Fuck 3.23 using Python 3.6.2, installed using pip,  and The Fuck 3.2 using Python 3.6.2 using dnf
* Independant of bash (Happens with GNU bash, version 4.4.12(1)-release (x86_64-redhat-linux-gnu) and zsh 5.3.1 (x86_64-redhat-linux-gnu))

What happens:

Create any rule that uses command.output, for example thefollowing bar.py, in ~/.config/thefuck/rules :

    def match(command):
        return ('fuck' in command.output)
    def get_new_command(command):
        return '{}'.format(command.script)

issue_title: Boolean Function Unknown Values
issue_body:
Currently, PyEDA only supports constant zero and one. There is value to extending the set of Boolean constants to include the following values:
- _logical_: either zero or one, but not both
- _illogical_: both zero and one

These two values can be used to optimistically or pessimistically evaluate functions. For example, `And(0, logical)` would always be `0`, but `And(0, illogical)` would always be `illogical`.

The encoding of _logical_ will be `2`, and the encoding of _illogical_ will be `3`.

The implementation needs to support this as an _optional_ feature in a function representation. It makes sense for expressions, but not for all representations. For example, implicant tables use positional cube notation to represent multi-valued logic, which has different usage and semantics.

issue_title: Support passing in a `Session` object
issue_body:
Users may already have a `requests.Session` object and wish to reuse that rather than having `httsleep` create a blank one.  This would save users from re-entering standard headers, settings etc. that they've already configured elsewhere.

### Ideal usage afterwards:
```python
    httsleep(url, until=until, alarms=alarms, session=my_session)
```

### ~~~Workaround~~~ Not usable
```python
    poller = HttSleeper(url, until=until, alarms=alarms)
    poller.session = my_session
    poller.run()
```
Not usable because [here](https://github.com/kopf/httsleep/blob/master/httsleep/main.py#L148) the request is prepared without the context of the Session:
```python
response = self.session.send(self.request.prepare()
```

The workaround would be usable if this line was instead:
```python
response = self.session.send(self.session.prepare_request(request))
```

issue_title: Monkeypatch socket DNS resolving functions
issue_body:
I recently became aware of the fact that functions like `socket.gethostbyname` and `socket.getaddrinfo` do not construct socket objects and so will not go through the proxy when the socket class is monkeypatched. For many applications this is not a problem, but anything that manually calls the DNS resolving functions may run into problems and will also suffer from a major privacy leak, as the DNS requests will be sent unproxied.

These functions are defined in C, in socketmodule.c and getaddrinfo.c. Currently trying to determine the best way of monkeypatching these. I'd prefer not to edit and compile the C extensions themselves.

issue_title: Reset or shrink momentum vector when shrinking learning rate
issue_body:
Not sure at all, but to me it looks like any momentum is preserved after learning rate is shrunk.

If so, then I guess as a result the optimizer might end up going in an unfavorable direction for quite a while, which may take a very long time to backtrack, given the new (smaller-than-before) learning rate.

I think it would make sense to reset or shrink also the momentum vector, whenever the learning rate is shrunk.

@davisking, can you comment if the above makes any sense? Just an observation from a single training run – didn't look in a lot of detail yet. If it already works like this, then I'm happy to close the issue right away.

issue_title: regression flag
Ray.Intersection and Mesh distance form an origin
issue_body:
Dear Mike, 

Thank you for the prompt reply on my previous question. I have another question which might again be pyembree related. You commented briefly a potential issue in the ray_pyembree.py module:
```
# based on an internal tolerance of embree?
# 1e-4 definetly doesn't work
_ray_offset_distance = .01
```

The problem I am facing is following. By creating a mesh with a large coordinates values and offsetting a ray origin from the mesh surface by 0.001 or 0.01 the ray.intersection_any reports intersection (True) despite the ray origin is offset. This does not happen in all cases. When move the same mesh close to the Cartesian origin (0,0,0) there is no intersection; which is correct. I created a simple example which shows this misbehaviour. The mesh has a single rectangle composed of two triangles. Here is the code:

```
import numpy as np
import trimesh


def unit_normal_vector(p1, p2, p3):
    x, y, z = np.cross((p2 - p1), (p3 - p1))
    magnitude = np.sqrt(np.square(x) + np.square(y) + np.square(z))
    unit_normal_vector = list(np.array([x, y, z]) / magnitude)
    return unit_normal_vector

# Mesh first vertice 528980.85 183526.1 0
mesh = trimesh.load_mesh('./test_mesh.obj')

# Extract coordinates and determine a plane's unit normal vector
p1o, p2o, p3o = mesh.vertices[0:3]
unv = unit_normal_vector(p1o, p2o, p3o)

# Centroid of a rectangle's bottom edge elevated by 1m from the bottom.
cen = np.mean([p1o, p3o], axis=0)
cen[2] = 1

# Ray origin offset from the mesh rectangle by 0.01 along the unit normal vector
ep = cen + 0.01 * np.array(unv)

# Intersection returns True
intersection = mesh.ray.intersects_any([ep], [unv])
print(intersection)

# Mesh moved towards Cartesian origin (First vertice 80.85 26.1 0)
mesh_moved = trimesh.load_mesh('./test_mesh_moved.obj')

# Extract coordinates and determine a plane's unit normal vector
p1m, p2m, p3m = mesh_moved.vertices[0:3]
unv_moved = unit_normal_vector(p1m, p2m, p3m)

# Centroid of a rectangle's bottom edge elevated by 1m from the bottom.
cen_moved = np.mean([p1m, p3m], axis=0)
cen_moved[2] = 1

# Ray origin offset from the mesh rectangle by 0.001 along the unit normal vector
ep_moved = cen_moved + 0.001 * np.array(unv_moved)

# Intersection returns False (as it should be)
intersection_moved = mesh_moved.ray.intersects_any([ep_moved], [unv_moved])
print(intersection_moved)
```

test_mesh.obj
```
v 528980.85 183526.1 0
v 528980.85 183526.1 3
v 528982.3 183523.55 0
v 528982.3 183523.55 3
f 2 1 3
f 2 3 4
```

test_mesh_moved.obj
```
v 80.85 26.1 0
v 80.85 26.1 3
v 82.3 23.55 0
v 82.3 23.55 3
f 2 1 3
f 2 3 4
```
Best regards,
Ivan
p.s. Thank you very much for your effort in developing the trimesh library. It is a great library which I've been using a lot recently.

issue_title: Support multiple records of the same type on an identifier
issue_body:
In [certbot](https://github.com/certbot/certbot), we use lexicon to create TXT records for a domain to prove to a SSL/TLS certificate authority that the user has control over that domain so the CA will give you a certificate which you can use to set up HTTPS. Due to the way [the protocol](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-acme-acme-09) works, we sometimes have to set multiple TXT records for a single domain.

I'm not sure if each of the providers we use have problems with creating multiple TXT records on a domain (although I know this works with your code for dnsimple), but they all have problems deleting it and immediately error out if there is more than 1 record for the domain:

* [cloudxns](https://github.com/AnalogJ/lexicon/blob/a8c478ad44c06012c3aaaac077f70e67c12a136e/lexicon/providers/cloudxns.py#L125)
* [dnsimple](https://github.com/AnalogJ/lexicon/blob/903af58378ab9942d817c57e0330b5f7ac26b4e9/lexicon/providers/dnsimple.py#L128)
* [dnsmadeeasy](https://github.com/AnalogJ/lexicon/blob/9ad8a9cbf9e4b5c24fe525b274966c2432baf132/lexicon/providers/dnsmadeeasy.py#L121)
* [luadns](https://github.com/AnalogJ/lexicon/blob/a8c478ad44c06012c3aaaac077f70e67c12a136e/lexicon/providers/luadns.py#L98)
* [nsone](https://github.com/AnalogJ/lexicon/blob/8b649338b6c03c7bfb2d465249cd759de62296cb/lexicon/providers/nsone.py#L175)

Can support for this be added?

issue_title: txtorcon > 0.19.0 is returning unicode instead of strings when calling TorProtocol.get_info()
issue_body:
Since 0.19.0 `get_info` commands will return unicode values instead of strings. It could be that other people would also be a bit surprised by this fact, so at the very leasts some note should be added to the changelog to mention this or ideally (if there not a good reason to do so) restore the old behavior of returning strings.

Here is a minimal way to reproduce the issue:

```
import txtorcon
config = txtorcon.TorConfig()
def updates(prog, tag, summary):
    print "%d%%: %s" % (prog, summary)
proto = yield txtorcon.launch_tor(config, reactor, progress_updates=updates)
s = txtorcon.TorState(proto.tor_protocol)
state = yield s.post_bootstrap
result = yield state.protocol.get_info("address")
assert isinstance(result.values()[0], str)
```

The above snippet will succeed in txtorcon 0.18.0, while fail in txtorcon >= 0.19.0

issue_title: RFC reduce user choices
issue_body:
Users tend to make dubious choices. I see poorly configured fishnet nodes on my monitoring.

For instance someone using one core of 12 threads. Stockfish is not good at this, and the analysis have lower PV and depth.

I think fishnet should enforce 4 threads max, or maybe 6. And maybe totally remove that setting from the installation wizard, as I'm sure fishnet will make better choices on its own, generally. Then remove the cores setting as well.
They would stay available as settings, just not in the install wizard.

Here's the sample python code:

var1, var2 = 1, 2

import jedi

var1, var2 = 1, 2

import jedi
source = '''
var1, var2 = 1, 2
print var1, var2
'''

issue_title: Dynamic Twisted Thread Pool
issue_body:
If you defer a number of things to thread each of which call EventualResult.wait() it is possible to fill the threadpool with blocked threads, causing hangs or timeouts.

It'd be great if EventualResult flagged the pool worker threads as blocked, allowing the pool to create new threads, and resolve the nested defer to threads.

issue_title: ERROR_BAD_LENGTH in winfile.GetFileInformationByHandleEx on x86 - a struct alignment issue.
issue_body:
In latests x86 builds(211), both 2.7 and 3.6, when called with [`FileBasicInfo`](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa364228.aspx), [`GetFileInformationByHandleEx`](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa364953.aspx) throws an exeption with winerror code `ERROR_BAD_LENGTH`:

Python 2.7.13 x86 with latest Pythonwin on windows 7sp1-64:
~~~
>>> from win32file import *; from win32con import *
>>> hfile = CreateFile(r'C:\Windows\notepad.exe', GENERIC_READ, FILE_SHARE_READ, None, OPEN_EXISTING, 0, None)
>>> GetFileInformationByHandleEx(hfile, FileBasicInfo)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<interactive input>", line 1, in <module>
error: (24, 'GetFileInformationByHandleEx', 'The program issued a command but the command length is incorrect.')
~~~

The cause of the error is a bug in `<winsock2.h>` included from `win32file.i`. 
Here's some cl.exe output with `/showIncludes` switch:
~~~
Note: including file: \WinSDK\Include\winsock2.h
Note: including file:  \WinSDK\Include\pshpack4.h << pragma pack(push, 4) here
Note: including file:  \WinSDK\Include\windows.h  << all Windows stuff gets compiled with non default alignment
Note: including file:   \WinSDK\Include\sdkddkver.h
...cut
Note: including file:  \WinSDK\Include\poppack.h
~~~
This bug can tracked to [2003](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/microsoft.public.vc.ide_general/C_IW_byLdQU), it's present in 7.0a SDK and is fixed already in 8.1.

Struct [`FILE_BASIC_INFO`](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa364217.aspx) contains four int64 fields and one int32 so its `sizeof` by default would be 40 bytes with padding, the odd packing directive decreases its size by omitting padding and `GetFileInformationByHandleEx` refuses to fill the struct.

The obvious fix would be to just include `windows.h` before `winsock2` but it's not [that easy](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/1372480) due to conflict between `winsock2` and `winsock`. The second option is to define `WIN32` macro before inclusion of `winsock2.h` but I don't know will it work with all supported SDKs.

I'll attach a patch later today.

Reported by: robyschek

Original Ticket: [pywin32/bugs/752](https://sourceforge.net/p/pywin32/bugs/752)

issue_title: Push tags to github
issue_body: It would be useful if releases were tagged and pushed as tags. There's a good chance you locally already do so, but haven't pushed them since git doesn't do this by default. You can use ```git push --tags``` or if you use annotated tags [use followTags](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/3745135/push-git-commits-tags-simultaneously/3745250#3745250) to automatically include them on ```git push```.

issue_title: freezetime breaks datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(time.time())
issue_body:
`datetime.datetime.today()` should be equivalent to `datetime.fromtimestamp(time.time())`. However in 0.3.7 I get this result:

```
>>> import datetime
>>> import time
>>> from freezegun import freeze_time
>>> with freeze_time(datetime.datetime(2016, 9, 9)):
...     print datetime.datetime.today()
...     print datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(time.time())
... 
2016-09-09 00:00:00
2016-09-09 08:00:00
```

It should be:

```
>>> import datetime
>>> import time
>>> print datetime.datetime.today(), datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(time.time())
2016-09-09 17:36:33.659690 2016-09-09 17:36:33.659747
>>> 
```

It looks like something wrong with the time zone.

issue_title: No way to provide a fixed serializer for `GET` method
issue_body:
I have API ViewSet that calls `get_objects_method` in `get_serializer_class`.

```python
from rest_framework import mixins
from rest_framework.viewsets import GenericViewSet


class MyViewSet(mixins.CreateModelMixin,
                mixins.RetrieveModelMixin,
                mixins.UpdateModelMixin,
                mixins.DestroyModelMixin,
                GenericViewSet):
    def get_serializer_class(self):
        obj = self.get_object()

        # custom logic here
```

It throws an excpetion:

```
AssertionError: Expected view MyViewSet to be called with a URL keyword argument named "pk". Fix your URL conf, or set the `.lookup_field` attribute on the view correctly.
```

According to [documentation](https://drf-yasg.readthedocs.io/en/stable/openapi.html#a-note-on-limitations) there are two ways to fix it:

1. Provide a fixed serializer for request and response body introspection using `@swagger_auto_schema`, to prevent `get_serializer` from being called on the view.
2. Exclude your endpoint from introspection.

But in my case first one is not possible to do because it will throw an error if I decorate `retrieve` method:

```
drf_yasg.errors.SwaggerGenerationError: request_body can only be applied to (PUT,PATCH,POST,DELETE); are you looking for query_serializer or manual_parameters?
```

Any ideas how to fix this or exclude viewset from schema generation is the only way?

## Breadcrumbs

1. `inspectors.view.SwaggerAutoSchema.get_default_responses`
2. `drf_yasg.inspectors.view.SwaggerAutoSchema.get_request_serializer`

issue_title: Serialization bug
issue_body:
Given the data:

    {'foo': ['bar:', 'baz']}

pyaml serializes this as:

    foo:
      - bar:
      - baz

which then gets deserialized by yaml as:

    {'foo': [{'bar': None}, 'baz']}

This is different to the original data. I'm not sure if this is a problem with pyaml, yaml, or something else. The problem is fixed by forcing quotes around "bar:" in the output:

    foo:
      - "bar:"
      - baz

This can be achieved by:

    pyaml.dump(data, stream, string_val_style="double-quoted")

Sample code to reproduce the problem:

```
import yaml
import pyaml
import StringIO
data = {"foo": ["bar:", "baz"]}
print data
stream = StringIO.StringIO()
pyaml.dump(data, stream)
print stream.getvalue()
data2 = yaml.load(stream.getvalue())
print data2
```

issue_title: Investigate flake8 as a lint checker
Ability to fill entry boxes if user does not
issue_body:
Original text from:
http://all-you-need-is-tech.blogspot.com/2013/01/improving-easygui-for-python.html


Andrew S08 June, 2013 23:32
Thanks Robbie this is very useful for building GUIs that look decent without much effort. One question: I have a multenterbox where one field can be left blank; if left blank, the program will autogenerate a number for the user. I would like to update the onscreen field with this number without closing the window (remain inside the callback). How can this be achieved?

Reply
Replies

Robbie Brook09 June, 2013 12:00
Hi Andrew,
What you're asking to do is actually populate the values in easygui.multenterbox after you sent them. This feature isn't supported.
I would go for a simple solution like:

    import easygui_callback 
    import random

    def get_auto_value():
        # your logic here
        return random.random()
    choices = ["name", "address", "auto-field"]
    values= ['default-name','default-address','']
    input= ''

    while True:
        input = easygui_callback.multenterbox("auto field","generator", choices,values)
        if input == None: break
    if input[2] == "":
        values= [input[0],input[1],get_auto_value()]
    else:
        # save data somewhere or whatever
        pass

issue_title: PrettyMIDI.adjust_times should force times to be monotonic
issue_body: Right now, there is no check/enforcement that `original_times` and `new_times` be monotonically increasing.  This results in very strange behavior, like negative tempi, which causes later issues.  They should be tested for monotonicity; if they are not monotonic, a warning should be given and they should be made monotonic with `np.maximum.accumulate`.

issue_title: Script not found: modifying PATH
issue_body:
If you install it with `pip install dhis2-pocket-knife --user` and then try to run any of the scripts, e.g. `dhis2-pk-share --help`, it may be that the script could not be found.

Fix:
You need to add it to your PATH in order to find it.

### MacOS
Replace `$USER` with your macOS username, then paste it into a terminal.
`echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/Users/$USER/Library/Python/2.7/bin' >> ~/.bash_profile && source ~/.bash_profile`

issue_title: Wrongly bump other package's version if they're in same file and version number are same with current package version
issue_body:
If current_version same with a requirement package's version in a meta file(like `setup.py`), bumpversion will wrongly bump requirments's version.

In following example, "package>=0.0.1" shouldn't be changed:

```bash
(yasaka)mitsukeru@Miku:~/project/test-proj$ bumpversion patch --dry-run --verbose
Reading config file .bumpversion.cfg:
[bumpversion]
files = setup.py test-proj/__init__.py
commit = True
tag = True
current_version = 0.0.1

Parsing version '0.0.1' using regexp '(?P<major>\d+)\.(?P<minor>\d+)\.(?P<patch>\d+)'
Parsed the following values: major=0, minor=0, patch=1
Attempting to increment part 'patch'
Values are now: major=0, minor=0, patch=2
Dry run active, won't touch any files.
New version will be '0.0.2'
Asserting files setup.py, test-proj/__init__.py contain the version string:
Found '0.0.1' in setup.py at line 11:     "package>=0.0.1"
Found '0.0.1' in test-proj/__init__.py at line 2: __version__ = '0.0.1'
Would change file setup.py:
--- a/setup.py
+++ b/setup.py
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@
 readme = open('README.rst').read()
 
 requirements = [
-    "package>=0.0.1"
+    "package>=0.0.2"
     # TODO: put package requirements here
 ]
 
@@ -19,7 +19,7 @@
 
 setup(
     name='test-proj',
-    version='0.0.1',
+    version='0.0.2',
     description='feature generators used inside BosonData.',
     long_description=readme,
     author='gestapolur',
Would change file test-proj/__init__.py:
--- a/test-proj/__init__.py
+++ b/test-proj/__init__.py
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
 # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
 
-__version__ = '0.0.1'
+__version__ = '0.0.2'
Would write to config file .bumpversion.cfg:
[bumpversion]
files = setup.py test-proj/__init__.py
commit = True
tag = True
current_version = 0.0.2
```

issue_title: Wrap all exceptions into a Regenmaschine variant
issue_body:
Right now, other than built-in Python exception types, Regenmaschine raises exceptions relating to the underlying HTTP library (Requests). After some time, I don't like this: it requires the implementer to import the library and its exceptions in order to catch them correctly.

Instead, even though it's an extra layer, we should coerce all exceptions into either a default or a Regenmaschine type.

> awslogs get [GROUP [STREAM_EXPRESSION]]

It would be nice, if `awslogs get` would retrieve all streams in all groups - allowing for `awslogs get --watch` to watch everything.

Additionally `GROUP` could also allow for (regular) expressions to specify multiple, but not all of them.

I have a position where I want to use an engine to do a shallow, broad analysis and return me centipawn scores. In this case, there are 32 legal moves in the position, and I wanted a depth 10 scoring of all the legal moves. When I tried this using python-chess, I found that the InfoHandler was capturing "info" lines from the engine on only 20 variations, even though the engine MultiPV was set to something larger. The below test-case and output should demonstrate the problem. I verified that if I send the corresponding UCI commands to the engine with 32 PVs, it does indeed output info strings for 32 variations; UCI code is further down at the bottom.

engine.uci()
engine.isready()

issue_title: vectors are parsed as mutable lists
issue_body:
Now, emitting mutable values may or may not be an issue depending on the point of view, but since you went to the trouble of implementing immutable dicts, you probably want them to be *completely* immutable.
```python
data = edn_format.loads('{5 [1 2 3]}')
print( type(data) ) # <class 'edn_format.immutable_dict.ImmutableDict'>
data[5].append(8)
print(data) # {5: [1, 2, 3, 8]}
```
...Also, Python lists are *unhashable*, so both "{[1 2 3] 5}" and "#{[1 2 3] 5}" generate *TypeError*.

Proposed solution is to add a corresponding class (named, for example, ImmutableVector), implemented in similar fashion to ImmutableDict; however, whether it extends *list* or not (it would make sense to implement it using tuples), it would break code that mutates parsed vectors.

Scrapy calls process_exception() when a download handler or a process_request() (from a downloader middleware) raises an exception (including an IgnoreRequest exception)

issue_title: Suggestion: Moving config to $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/powerline-shell/config.json
issue_body: How about moving the configuration file from `$HOME/powerline-shell.json` to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/powerline-shell/config.json` or simply `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/powerline-shell.json`, to adhere to the [XDG Base Directory Specification](https://standards.freedesktop.org/basedir-spec/latest/)?

issue_title: Add boundary handlers for stuck particles
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
There are some stuck particles as reported in #234 and #150 . This is related to `compute_position` waiving position updates whenever a particle will potentially end up out-of-bounds. There are standard ways to avoid this problem. The reflective bounds is one option, but the related PR is currently stale, so it may be important to create a general framework for handling the boundary problem.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Create a set of handlers, such as a `BoundaryHandler`. Usage example:

```python
# Usage outside of the class
from pyswarms.backend import operators
from pyswarms.backend.handlers import BoundaryHandler

bh = BoundaryHandler(strategy="reflective") # And other arguments here if it's parameterized
# We might have different handlers in the future
# so maybe passing them as a list may be good
ops.compute_position(swarm, handlers=[bh]) 
```

```python
# Inside BoundaryHandler
class BoundaryHandler(object):
    def __call__(): # parameterized
        # Some if-else logic here depending on the type of handler, for example:
        if self.strategy == "reflective":
            new_positions = self.reflective() # parameterized
```

issue_title: Second argument to context.parallelize is numSlices, not numPartitions
issue_body: I have no idea why pyspark uses different terminology, but the optional second argument to parallelize is `numSlices`, not `numPartitions`. See: https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/api/python/pyspark.html#pyspark.SparkContext.parallelize

issue_title: use class style instead of function style
sdist tarball is missing some files and dirs
issue_body:
The tarball uploaded to PyPI via the setup.py sdist command is missing some of the content in the repo because there's no `MANIFEST.in` file. This content includes the `docs` and `examples` subfolders, the `LICENSE` and `Changelog` files and the `requirement-*.txt` files.
Here's the `MANIFEST.in` file that you can add to include them:
```
include *.txt Changelog LICENSE Makefile
include docs/conf.py
recursive-include docs *.rst
graft examples
```
This will make packager's life much easier as the tarball will contain all we need.

issue_title: Temporary user backend
issue_body:
Is there any existing backend to support a "temporary" user? This isn't a good word for it -- let me explain what I'm looking for:

While I want information a given user enters to be stored in a user record, and for them to be treated as if they are logged in, I don't want to require them to log in with a social account or to make a password. They'd be remembered as logged in, and so associated to their row in the users table for now, via session or cookie. While still logged in they have the option to register (i.e. set a password to go with their email address, or log in to a social account to become associated) so they can log back in later. If they don't do this their record would still be stored but they (probably?) wouldn't be able to become reassociated with it at a later time.

I suspect I may be able to use the "email" backend for this and just not set a password, since I'll be collecting email addresses anyway. What I'm not clear on is how feasible this is, and whether I'd then easily be able to allow the user to add a password or associate a social account with the already-logged-in account.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

issue_title: Feature Request: Optional flag to use 3.1.5 way of doing command execution
issue_body:
## Detailed issue explanation

Pentesting from a restrictive environment sometimes doesn't allow ingress on port 445 on the attack machine. The 4.0 version of CME seems to throw an SMB server up for each exec method (wmiexec, smbexec, etc.) which I'm failing to get an output from commands due to CME waiting to get information back through 445.

I noticed in the debug output of 3.1.5 that it doesn't throw an SMB server up. Is it possible for you to add a flag like "--old-exec" to use the 3.1.5 method of executing commands?

issue_title: loads_with_timestamp
issue_body:
Hi guys,

I was debugging some issues I'm having with `django-password-reset` and noticed that the `RecoverDone` view makes a call to `loads_with_timestamp` function defined right above it: https://github.com/brutasse/django-password-reset/blob/0.7/password_reset/views.py#L24

The docstring states that the function should return the unsigned value along with its timestamp, but there's a try/except in case `signing.SignatureExpired` exception doesn't occur, `None` would be returned which means that the view will fail returning a `TypeError` exception because it would be trying to iterate over `None` to assign `timestamp` and `email` in the context.

Thought that there might be some inconsistency there.

issue_title: What if the frame length is greater than NFFT?
issue_body:
I'm not an expert in this kind of stuff, so I'm sorry if this will be a waste of time.

From the [numpy.fft.rfft documentation](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/reference/generated/numpy.fft.rfft.html) [in our case: n=NFTT, input=frame]:
"Number of points along transformation axis in the input to use. **If n is smaller than the length of the input, the input is cropped.** If it is larger, the input is padded with zeros. If n is not given, the length of the input along the axis specified by axis is used."

Is not this cropping something we want to avoid? Because, as far as I've seen, there's not any check in the code about how the frame size compares to NFTT.

issue_title: document.save() does not return response data indicating new _rev
issue_body:
## Bug Description

### 1. Steps to reproduce and the simplest code sample possible to demonstrate the issue

This is a request for an extension (backwards compatible).

The `document.save()` method performs a PUT operation on the document. According to the [Cloudant API reference for Documents](https://console.bluemix.net/docs/services/Cloudant/api/document.html#documents), the response to the PUT operation contains data like in the following example:
```
{
    "ok":true,
    "id":"apple",
    "rev":"2-9176459034"
}
```

The important piece of information in that response is the new revision of the updated document. 

Having the new revision at hand significantly simplifies the algorithm to resolve server-side conflicts: The returned new revision can easily be checked against the conflict revisions returned when specifying query parameter `conflicts=true`, to find out whether a successful update lost or won a server-side conflict.

However, the response data is not returned in the current implementation of the `Document.save()` method:

```
    def save(self):
        """
        . . .
        """
        headers = {}
        headers.setdefault('Content-Type', 'application/json')
        if not self.exists():
            self.create()
            return
        put_resp = self.r_session.put(
            self.document_url,
            data=self.json(),
            headers=headers
        )
        put_resp.raise_for_status()
        data = put_resp.json()
        super(Document, self).__setitem__('_rev', data['rev'])
        return   # <-- No response data returned, currently
```

This could easily and backwards compatibly be added by returning the response data as a json object (= dict):

```
    def save(self):
        . . .
        data = put_resp.json()
        super(Document, self).__setitem__('_rev', data['rev'])
        return data    # <-- Response data returned
```

### 2. What you expected to happen

Make the new revision that is already returned in the HTTP response of a document update available to the callers of the `Document.save()` method.

Update the docstring of the method, accordingly.

### 3. What actually happened

`Document.save()` returns `None`, and the returned new revision is not surfaced to the caller.

## Environment details
- Version(s) that are affected by this issue.
    > 2.9.0
- Python version
    > 2.7.14

issue_title: Is it possible to support 5-dim or x-dim data operation
issue_body:
Presently, we are use 4-dim data flow to build most of our models, **BatchSize x W x H x Channels** or **BatchSize x Channels x H x W**.

Is it possible to build 5-dim data flow? Maybe **BatchSize x Capsule x W x H x Channels**, which should be useful in ideas like CapsNet for that we don't have to reshape out data. So we can build high dimensions models which are more complicated and should be better both in structure and data extraction performance. 

According to **MobileNetV2**, performance of relu  is better in high dimensions than in low dimensions. So it maybe even more better if we can build our entire model with high dimensions operations. 
Further more, how about 6-dim or 7-dim operations, even x-dim operations?

Is this kind of operations possible? If possible, how hard will it be to implement them?

issue_title: Tasks that complete in <1s may display as RUNNING or PENDING in task history
issue_body:
Using MySQL backend, I am seeing tasks that have completed in either "RUNNING" or "PENDING" state in the task_history webpage.  Looking at the db there is a row marking these tasks as "DONE" but their timestamps clash with the displayed event.  Clearly the timestamps aren't sub-second precision.

E.g.

    mysql> select id, event_name, ts from task_events where task_id=61;
    +-----+------------+---------------------+
    | id  | event_name | ts                  |
    +-----+------------+---------------------+
    | 181 | PENDING    | 2015-04-22 15:10:02 |
    | 230 | RUNNING    | 2015-04-22 15:12:38 |
    | 231 | DONE       | 2015-04-22 15:12:38 |
    +-----+------------+---------------------+
    3 rows in set (0.00 sec)

Modern MySQL should support this.  I have found this stackoverflow question suggesting the issue is with SQLAlchemy: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/29711102/sqlalchemy-mysql-millisecond-or-microsecond-precision

Other than fixing this in SQLAlchemy it might work to simply sort task_event rows by timestamp *and* primary key, assuming the events are inserted in true order.

issue_title: Visualizer fails with similar range targets.
issue_body:
I'm using Range Targets to guide the loading of some information that appears on a daily basis that we want to load ASAP. (We have the configuration set to retry on external task failures and to keep the workers alive)

When you run two Range Targets with the same parameters at the same time, but the target task has different parameters, the visualizer will only display one of the range targets in any given screen. The sub-tasks are of course still present and Luigi doesn't appear to have any issue with the Range targets being identical, so I am presuming this is an okay way to use Range. My command lines look something like this:

```

```

Am I just going about this backassward? I feel like this would be an issue if you had to run a range on a parameterized task for any reason. Just to reiterate, there's *no* problem with the jobs running or anything, this only shows up as an issue in the visualizer.

issue_title: Dependencies not installed when installing Paver project via "python setup.py install"
issue_body:
Example **pavement.py** file with dependencies (_plumbum_, for example) declared via "install_requires":

``` python
from paver.setuputils import setup

setup(
name="TEST",
version="0.1",
description="TESTS",
author="Jane Doe",
author_email="none@example.net",
packages=[""],
install_requires=["plumbum"]
)
```

Generate **setup.py** and minilib:

```
$ paver generate_setup
---> paver.misctasks.generate_setup
Write setup.py
$ paver minilib
---> paver.misctasks.minilib
Generate paver-minilib.zip
```

Now try to install it in a virtualenv by invoking `python setup.py install` directly from the project directory:

```
$ mkdir test_venv
$ cd test_venv
$ virtualenv .
Running virtualenv with interpreter /usr/bin/python2
New python executable in ./bin/python2
Also creating executable in ./bin/python
Installing setuptools, pip...done.
$ . bin/activate
$ cd ..
$ python setup.py install
[ output suppressed, looks normal enough though ]
Installed /tmp/pavertest/plumbum-1.4.2-py2.7.egg
[ output suppressed ]
```

As you can see, it does declare that it installed _plumbum_. However, looking at `lib/python2.7/site-packages` inside the virtualenv directory, it isn't there. The corresponding .egg file is present in the project directory, however.

Can anyone reproduce this so far? Am I doing something wrong?

---
## Possible causes

As part of the installation, Paver's [`setuputils._dispatch_setuptools_install`](https://github.com/paver/paver/blob/c66f3183d01185ea877df1502a5aee7d34976f7b/paver/setuputils.py#L44) is called, which in turn calls setuptools' `fetch_build_eggs`.
But this seems to only download the eggs, not install them.

The requirements aren't installed by setuptools' own install command because [those people actually built a mechanism that detects where `install` was called from](https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/src/6cf39105a5b9/setuptools/command/install.py#cl-63) and does an "old-style" installtion (i.e. the way distutils does it, without installing dependencies) if it wasn't called from inside setuptools itself. I suppose that's why `_dispatch_setuptools_install` was created in the first place; it just doesn't work.

issue_title: PyAnnotate ignores existing type annotations for partially annotated functions
issue_body:
If a function's arguments are annotated, but return type is missing, for example:
```
def foo(
    arg1,  # type: Optional[int]
    arg2,  # type: Optional[str]
):
    return "result"
```
PyAnnotate ignores existing annotations, and adds a separate one:
```
def foo(
    arg1,  # type: Optional[int]
    arg2,  # type: Optional[str]
):
    # type: (None, None) -> str
    return "result"
```

Moreover, if we have more arguments,
```
def foo(
    argument1,  # type: Optional[int]
    argument2,  # type: Optional[str]
    argument3,  # type: Optional[str]
    argument4,  # type: Optional[str]
    argument5,  # type: Optional[str]
    argument6,  # type: Optional[str]
):
    return "result"
```

is annotated as
```
def foo(
    argument1,  # type: None  # type: Optional[int]
    argument2,  # type: None  # type: Optional[str]
    argument3,  # type: None  # type: Optional[str]
    argument4,  # type: None  # type: Optional[str]
    argument5,  # type: None  # type: Optional[str]
    argument6,  # type: None  # type: Optional[str]
    ):
    # type: (...) -> str
    return "result"
```

requests = "*"
pywinusb = {version = "*", sys_platform = "== 'win32'"}
```

However, the text refers to [PEP 508](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0508/) which does not have win32 as a sys_platform. According to [PEP 496](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0496/) it reads:

> After this PEP was initially drafted, PEP 508 was developed and submitted to fully specify the dependency declaration syntax, including environment markers. As a result, this PEP ended up being rejected in favor of the more comprehensive PEP 508.

As such, shouldn't PEP 508 also be used in the code example?

##### Expected result
I expected the code example to read:

```sh
[packages]
requests = "*"
pywinusb = {version = "*", platform_system= "== 'Windows'"}
```

Note that I understand that `sys_platform = "== 'win32'` works, but it was confusing not finding win32 in the PEP 508 documentation.

##### Actual result

n/a

##### Steps to replicate

n/a

issue_title: Runtime warning about missing wheel support
issue_body:
setuptools should issue a giant warning that it does not support installing wheels when all potentially matching distributions in a PyPI result (or other collection of installation sources) are wheels. If it does not get to this point because it excludes all wheels first and then ends up with an empty set of distributions, it might as well warn then, too.

Currently, all the user gets to see is this:

    > python setup.py install
    [...]
    Processing dependencies for [internal package name elided]
    Searching for PyQt5>=5.7
    Reading https://pypi.python.org/simple/PyQt5/
    No local packages or working download links found for PyQt5>=5.7
    error: Could not find suitable distribution for Requirement.parse('PyQt5>=5.7')

There is no indication whatsoever of _why_ no suitable distribution is found. A look at the PyPI web site will show at least one matching package, and pip _will_ install it. For a user who does not know about the lack of wheel support in setuptools, it is then not exactly trivial to find out why this command does not work.

Googling the error message does not (at this time) return any helpful hits. Not even googling "setuptools wheel" does, and this query is far more targeted, if very unlikely to occur to an unaware user.

Finally, even for a user who is familiar with the wheel package format, it is not obvious at all that setuptools cannot _install_ wheels, when it is the centrally important tool for _building_ them.

Perhaps setuptools could delegate installation to pip instead of easy_install.

issue_title: setup() creating broken PKG-INFO if using unicode_literals in Python 2.7
issue_body:
If I use `from __future__ import unicode_literals` in `setup.py`, the `keywords` parameter will be broken into a list of comma-separated characters in PKG-INFO when I invoke `python setup.py register`.

Example:

    keyword='multiprocessing parallel workers multiworker'

will result in the following line in PKG-INFO:

    Keywords: m,u,l,t,i,p,r,o,c,e,s,s,i,n,g, ,p,a,r,a,l,l,e,l, ,w,o,r,k,e,r,s, ,m,u,l,t,i,w,o,r,k,e,r

The workaround is to specify it as `bytes`, e.g., `b"enter some keywords"`.

So, this bug has a workaround, but kind of annoying to have to remember that.

I am using **Python 2.7.11** with **setuptools 20.6.7**

issue_title: MessageTooLarge uncaught failure using rdkafka
issue_body:
Note that I am using SNAPPY compression on the producer.

Has anyone attempted to send a 2mb+ message to kafka with rdkafka turned on? I noticed that it puts an exception in the delivery report queue that states that the message is too large, but doesnt throw an exception at all. It just skips sending that message

With rdkafka turned off it posts to kafka just fine, which makes me think that rdkafka is either not compressing the message OR its not using the "compressed size" of the message to compare to kafka's message.max.bytes setting. Which is currently at the default of 1000000.

So what i think may be happening is without rdfkafka the pykafka library is compressing the 2mb message below the default setting and not throwing an exception, while the rdkafka producer is NOT compressing the message prior to checking that value.

issue_title: Kafka consumer partition offset off by 2?
issue_body:
Hi,

I have a kafka topic with a single partition. I would like to create a consumer with the single purpose of returning me the last message that was produced on the channel (and that's it).

The use case is that this is run by the producer at the beginning of each run. The producer listens to some other data source and relays any new entries into the kafka topic. In the case of producer downtime or failures, it needs to recover from where it last left off.

Please let me know if any of the below are not the correct ways to do this. This is basically what I have:

    def getKafkaTopic(topic):
        client = KafkaClient(zookeeper_hosts=getZookeeperHosts())
        return client.topics[topic]

and then

    topic = getKafkaTopic('some.topic.name')
    consumer = topic.get_simple_consumer(consumer_group="some_consumer_group")
    partitions = [p for p in consumer.partitions.itervalues()]
    offset_pairs = [(p, p.latest_available_offset() - 2) for p in partitions]
    consumer.reset_offsets(partition_offsets=offset_pairs)

This works. However, I'm baffled at the requirement for `p.latest_available_offset() - 2`.

Given that an offset value in kafka points to the "next topic to be consumed", I would expect a consumer at `p.latest_available_offset()` to block until the next message is produced, so that would be as expected. However, `p.latest_available_offset() - 1` will also block until the next message is produced, whereas `p.latest_available_offset() - 2` will get me the last message produced.

Is this an off by one error somewhere in pykafka? Or am I just not understanding kafka?

Thanks,
Jack

**PyKafka version**: 2.2.0
**Kafka version**: 0.8.2

issue_title: Support Storm's custom serialization support for multi-lang. [msgpack]
issue_body:
A recently [merged pull request 84 on the Storm project][merged] implements custom serialization support for the multi-lang protocol. This means that you can technically register other faster serializers than JSON for Storm's tuple data, e.g. we could use [msgpack][msgpack].

In addition, thanks to Storm's built-in support for serialization hooks with Kyro, we could actually switch Tuple serialization from Kyro's built-in JVM-based serializer to something like msgpack. This would potentially allow us to avoid the encode/decode cost associated with using ShellBolts and ShellSpouts.

It would be great for someone from the community to do a prototype of this using a streamparse project, and prove its viability. Then we could merge the support into the core library, build a support library in Java for the custom serializers, and offer documentation for how to set up Storm to leverage them. This will likely fit in very well with our lein-based Storm project management.

[merged]: https://github.com/apache/incubator-storm/pull/84
[msgpack]: http://msgpack.org/

issue_title: The TM benchmark doesn't cover many scenarios
issue_body:
Currently the TM benchmark just does 2000 steps of hotgym [1]. 

It should cover more scenarios. It should test various sequences, some that are very predictable and others that are not very predictable. And it should test the TM with various parameters, especially with various cellsPerColumn.

[1] And it's a little goofy: It gets the column SDR using a `RandomDistributedScalarEncoder` that only has `n=400`, so 1648 of the 2048 columns are never active.

issue_title: templates: slimmer facet UI
issue_body:
Currently, the search results UI uses "fat" facets:

![2015-09-24-172251_2560x1440_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/517546/10077551/04b22f4e-62e1-11e5-8d4e-28d78e1c6a57.png)

The facets are "overdecorated", they occupy too much visible screen space, diluting attention of the user from the main page body.  Moreover, the user cannot see more one facet from the get-go, making navigation harder.

Usually, the facets are presented in a slimmer manner, see for example RERODOC:

![2015-09-24-172554_2560x1440_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/517546/10077635/764887b6-62e1-11e5-9708-3d2930135e0a.png)

This improves the UX and resembles more closely to what people are used to on the Internet (e.g. Amazon).

@drjova Can you please look at amending facet's CSS to make them slimmer by default?  CC-ing also @kaplun @jmartinm who were looking at facet usability on INSPIRE, and @nharraud who were improving facet mechanism in the past (https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/pull/3400).

P.S. See also https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/issues/3415 and other issues on this topic.

issue_title: `missing_value` doesn't work for Int fields
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.schema/+bug/98479, @webmaven reported:

> The following creates an error:
```python
page_ordinal = zope.schema.Int(
    title = _(u'Ordinal'),
    description=_(u'Order of the pages'),
    default=0,
    missing_value=0, #don't know why this doesn't work
    )
```
> The error is as follows:
```
ConfigurationError: ('Invalid value for', 'class', "(None, (<type 'int'>, <type 'long'>))")
```

@mgedmin followed-up:

> I've looked briefly at this error. The error is raised because the defaults for `min_value` and `max_value` are both `None`. This fails type checking, because `None` is not an int or a long. When `missing_value` is None, type checking is skipped.

> I have to admit that I do not understand the semantics of `missing_value`.

> I think that Int should allow `min_value` and `max_value` to be None regardless of the `missing_value` setting. Nones should be treated as minus infinity and plus infinity respectively.

@webmaven replied:

> As I understand it, the value of `missing_value` is supposed to be used when the submitted form-field is empty. As opposed to `default_value`, which is supposed to pre-populate the form field.

@benji-york opined:

> That's how it seems to me too. I looked briefly at the widget tests and couldn't figure out how to test it, so will have to look later (unless someone beats me to it, hint, hint).

issue_title: HTTP_AUTHORIZATION is None
issue_body:
hello,

I am running into issue where I want to make a raw ajax request from my (ember) client on the user settings page (user must be authenticated).

```
  passwordSaveHandler: (caller, onSuccess, onError)->
    ...
    request = Ember.$.ajax
      url: 'v1/auth/password_reset'
      type: 'POST'
      data: data
      contentType: 'application/json'

    request.done (response)=>
      onSuccess()

    request.fail (response)=>
      onError()
```

The request is made and I see `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` header properly sent (yes the name is right). However I am then getting issue in my list_route()
```
    def _password_reset_handler(self, request):
        "Handles the actual password reset."
        old = request.data.get('oldPassword')
        new1 = request.data.get('newPassword1')
        new2 = request.data.get('newPassword2')
        if old and check_password(old, self.request.user.password):
            if new1 and new2 and new1 == new2:
                self.request.user.set_password(new1)
                return Response(_('success'), status=status.HTTP_200_ok)
            raise exc.BadRequest(_('The passwords did not match.'))
        raise exc.BadRequest(_('Could not verify password.'))

    @list_route(methods=['POST'], permission_classes=[IsAuthenticated])
    def password_reset(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
        """Resets a users password..
        Handles verifying the new passwords match and saving them as new.
        """
        return self._password_reset_handler(request)
```
where `self.request.user` == `AnonymousUser`

I've tested things out and got down to determining that in the request, `HTTP_AUTHORIZATION` is empty and thus no user is ever returned or authenticated!

Not sure what is causing this but any info appreciated!

issue_title: pyjnius not compatible with uwsgi
issue_body:
I've written a Flask app, it contains pyjnius to support invoking functionalities in third-party jar package. I've found pyjnius will be hang on in vast concurrent requests come in while using uwsgi+nginx to host.

Below is what I know for now:
let's say `/hello` is the normal endpoint and `/jnius` is the endpoint which will invoke jar package code. pyjnius setup is executed before the flask `app.run()`.
1. Using Flask alone or Flask+uwsgi+nginx or Flask+nginx to host, sending requests to endpoint `/hello`, all the things are fine. Also ok if I use `jpype` instead of `pyjnius`
2. Using Flask alone or Flask+nginx, pyjnius works fine when I use 100 threads to send 30k requests to endpoint `/jnius`. the whole flask app will crash if use `jpype` instead of `pyjnius`.
3. Using Flask+uwsgi+nginx, the whole application will hang up after handling about 100+ requests to endpoint `jnius`, and the following request to any endpoint will also hang up to timeout. 

Any idea about the explanation?

This app is used in the production environment, I can either use Flask alone (this will heavily decrease the throughout the app could handle which is not ok for production environment) or try to write another java program to handle the things about jar package and communicate with python code.

issue_title: Config with object that `_takes_rng`
issue_body:
I tried to test using a config with `RandomWalk`, which has `_takes_rng=True` set.

I initially created the following configuration
```python
    gal_config = {
        'type':'RandomWalk',
        'npoints':100,
        'half_light_radius':2.0,
    }
    config={
        'gal':gal_config,
    }
```

An exception is raised because the `rng` is missing.  This surprised me, because `rng` is an optional parameter
```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test_randwalk.py", line 253, in <module>
    test_randwalk_config()
  File "/home/esheldon/git/GalSim/tests/galsim_test_helpers.py", line 456, in f2
    result = f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "test_randwalk.py", line 199, in test_randwalk_config
    rw_from_config = galsim.config.BuildGSObject(config, 'gal')
  File "/home/esheldon/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/galsim/config/gsobject.py", line 182, in BuildGSObject
    gsobject, safe = _BuildSimple(param, base, ignore, gsparams, logger)
  File "/home/esheldon/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/galsim/config/gsobject.py", line 256, in _BuildSimple
    raise ValueError("No base['rng'] available for type = %s"%type_name)
ValueError: No base['rng'] available for type = RandomWalk

```

Note `flux` is not being set above, it is optional, yet no error is raised.

This goes away if I instead add the `rng` to the _outer_ config
```python
    gal_config = {
        'type':'RandomWalk',
        'npoints':100,
        'half_light_radius':2.0,
        'flux':np.pi,
    }
    config={
        'gal':gal_config,
        'rng':galsim.BaseDeviate(),
    }
```

So I have two questions:  

1. Should the `rng` be dealt with like other optional parameters?
2. Must the rng always be at the outer config?

issue_title: Extra vars, why force using a file ?
issue_body:
Why we can't pass extra vars as arguments ? It would ease things : 

Jenkins launches the build
Jenkins must create a file containing extra vars which will be read by tower-cli 
Jenkins calls tower-cli with `tower-cli tower-cli job launch --job-template 18 --extra-vars ex.yml`
Jenkins must erease the file

VS 

Jenkins launches the build
Jenkins calls tower-cli with `tower-cli tower-cli job launch --job-template 18 --extra-vars test:23,site:example.com`

issue_title: Support x-patternProperties
issue_body:
### Description

Would be nice to be able to define that. It is not currently supported by Swagger though.


### Expected bahaviour

Be able to define something like:
```yaml
  Labels:
    type: object
    description: |
      State of labels of a resource.
    x-patternProperties:
      "^[\\w_-]+$":
        type: string
        description: |
          Value of the tag.
    example:
      stage: prod
```

And Connexion would remove the `x-` from `x-patternProperties` property before validating the JSON schema.

### Actual behaviour

Everything under `x-patternProperties` is ignored.

issue_body:
myproxy/client.py

        if six.PY3:
            cert_dn_list.sort()
            self.__certDN = [(bytes(i[0], 'utf-8'), bytes(i[1],'utf-8'))
                             for i in cert_dn_list]
        else:
            self.__certDN = cert_dn_list.sort()

sets self.__certDN to None because cert_dn_list.sort() returns None. It should be:

        cert_dn_list.sort()
        if six.PY3:

issue_title: Just-in-time parameter updates
issue_body:
I noticed in the DyNet paper (https://arxiv.org/pdf/1701.03980v1.pdf) on page 19 that just-in-time parameter updates are mentioned, and Pegasos is cited. Two notes:

1 - My understanding is that AdaGrad does actually require an update to weights with zero gradient. However, they can also be implemented just-in-time as described in [http://aclweb.org/anthology/D/D15/D15-1032.pdf](http://aclweb.org/anthology/D/D15/D15-1032.pdf).
2 - I haven't seen it anywhere, but by approximating epsilon as 0 in Adam, I think a closed form update for weights with gradient zero can be formed.

Is there any interest in adding this to DyNet? I'm guessing it would be of the "we'd accept a pull request" variety :) but whether it is worth implementing depends on the anticipated impact on speed.

issue_title: PubSub: pull doesn't return messages on non-empty subscription
issue_body:
pubsub: 0.27.0

Our pubsub puller process encounters erratic behavior since beginning of December.

It runs a loop which looks like:
```python
while True:
      pulled = subscription.pull(return_immediately=False, max_messages=1)
      process_messages(pulled)
```

It used to work perfectly, but for a few weeks pull() sometimes returns, after ~600s, an empty list while there are still tens of messages on the subscription (as stated by stackdriver monitoring). 
At some points, it can take hours, the messages end up processed.
New incoming messages are sometimes pulled directly while old ones are still not delivered.

We didn't change anything in our code (same Docker image) for two months.

issue_title: Add Support for AuthorizationType to API
issue_body:
API Endpoints often require authorization permissions, e.g. only IAM users. Ideally, AWS::Serverless::Api would also support AuthorizationType available for API Gateway.

For example, using IAM user:

```
Events:
  Type: Api
  Properties:
    Path: /secrets
    Method: get
    AuthorizationType: AWS_IAM
```

Or a custom authorizer:

```
Events:
  Type: Api
  Properties:
    Path: /secrets
    Method: get
    AuthorizationType: CUSTOM
    Authorizer: <ARN_CUSTOM_AUTHORIZER>
```

Realize this may be challenging in referencing the custom authorizer function if not a function defined within the template though.

events.py starting at line 129:

if value.startswith('<DIDL-Lite'):

		else:
			value = from_didl_string(value)[0]
`

        restricted = element.get('restricted', None)

	restricted = True if restricted in [1, 'true', 'True'] else False

* `ipwb` returns the ipwb help docs of the CLI, also available via `ipwb help`
* `ipwb index` returns the help docs (params) needed to index a (W)ARC, also available via `ipwb index help`

* `ipwb replay` starts the replay system

Unable to set memory_limit and memswap_limit in host_config, both are missing from supported parameters in utils.py.

Containers created using python client,

	[{
	        "Config":  {
	        ...
	        "Memory": 2147483648,
	        "MemorySwap": -1,
	       ...
	       "HostConfig": {
	        ...
	        "Memory": 0,
	        "MemorySwap": 0,
	        ...

Containers created using commandline with --memory=2048MB --memory_swap=-1

	[{
	        "Config":  {
	        ...
	        "Memory": 2147483648,
	        "MemorySwap": -1,
	       ...
	       "HostConfig": {
	        ...
	        "Memory": 2147483648,
	        "MemorySwap": -1,
	        ...

As results, limits are only configured in Config and not Host_Config and therefore make no effect.

issue_title: Silent "Zip created at" message
issue_body:
I am not sure of use of this message - the filename is already known to the user.
When using the tool with parallel makefiles getting this message does not make sense.

Although it is easy to disable it by redirecting the output to /dev/null, it would be good to get it fixed in the tool itself.

Is it possible to show this message only if --verbose is specified.
Another option is --silent to suppress this message.

If needed I can submit a patch.

issue_title: Incorporate NI-DMM system tests into NI internal Automated Test Suite
issue_body:
NI maintains an Automated Test Suite for the driver.
It needs to install the corresponding nimi-python package and run the corresponding system tests as part of its execution.

This way we can ensure that future versions of the driver work with nimi-python.

issue_title: Incorrect types in samp_quant_samp_per_chan and total_samp_per_chan_generated parameters
issue_body:
Both samp_quant_samp_per_chan  and total_samp_per_chan_generated parameters
use c_double types, rather than c_long_long (UInt64) as required by the dll function.
Changing line 949 and 957 in out_stream.py and 2230, 2238 and 2253 in timing.py restores correct operation.

issue_title: `tuple index out of range` if raising ApplicationError without args
issue_body:
Simplest example:
```
from autobahn.twisted.wamp import ApplicationSession
from autobahn.wamp import ApplicationError
from twisted.internet.defer import inlineCallbacks


class App(ApplicationSession):
    def test(self):
        raise ApplicationError(u'error')

    @inlineCallbacks
    def onJoin(self, details):
        yield self.register(self.test, u'test')
        yield self.call(u'test')
```
`tuple index out of range` appears in line `errmsg = u"{0}".format(err.value.args[0])` (file autobahn/wamp/protocol.py)

issue_title: Give Value Distinctions for ambiguous Braille
issue_body:
1. from music21 import *
2. ambiguous = converter.parse("tinynotation: 4/4 C2 D4.. C32 C")
3. print braille.translate.objectToBraille(ambiguous)

**What is the expected output?**

⠀⠀⠀⠀⠼⠙⠲⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
⠼⠁⠀⠸⠝⠱⠄⠄⠠⠣⠂⠝⠝⠣⠅

or

⠀⠀⠀⠀⠼⠙⠲⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
⠼⠁⠀⠘⠣⠂⠸⠝⠱⠄⠄⠝⠝⠣⠅

**What do you see instead?**

⠀⠀⠀⠀⠼⠙⠲⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
⠼⠁⠀⠸⠝⠱⠄⠄⠝⠝⠣⠅


**Please provide any additional information below.**

Braille music note and rest values are inherently ambiguous.  The dot pattern
for a whole note can also mean a 16th note, a half note/rest can also mean a
32th note/rest and so on.  When a measure contains values like half and 32th,
value distinction signs need to be added in appropriate places to make
the values of all notes/rests clear to the reader.

In our example above, currently, the reader has absolutely no idea which of
the three C notes is the half note, and which are the 32ths.  The time signature
implies that one of them must be a half, but it is not possible to know which.

Note that there are possibly several solutions to a particular value ambiguity.
The shortest and most clear version should be choosen automatically.

issue_title: Improve foreign keys API
issue_body:
# Proposed API

The library supports foreign keys described in the [Table Schema](http://specs.frictionlessdata.io/table-schema/#foreign-keys) specification. It means if your data package descriptor use `resources[].schema.foreignKeys` property for some resources a data integrity will be checked on reading operations.

Consider we have a data package:

```python
DESCRIPTOR = {
  'resources': [
    {
      'name': 'teams',
      'data': [
        ['id', 'name', 'city'],
        ['1', 'Arsenal', 'London'],
        ['2', 'Real', 'Madrid'],
        ['3', 'Bayern', 'Munich'],
      ],
      'schema': {
        'fields': [
          {'name': 'id', 'type': 'integer'},
          {'name': 'name', 'type': 'string'},
          {'name': 'city', 'type': 'string'},
        ],
        'foreignKeys': [
          {
            'fields': 'city',
            'reference': {'resource': 'cities', 'fields': 'name'},
          },
        ],
      },
    }, {
      'name': 'cities',
      'data': [
        ['name', 'country'],
        ['London', 'England'],
        ['Madrid', 'Spain'],
      ],
    },
  ],
}
```

Let's check relations for a `teams` resource:

```python
from datapackage import Package

package = Package(DESCRIPTOR)
teams = package.get_resource('teams')
teams.check_relations()
# tableschema.exceptions.RelationError: Foreign key "['city']" violation in row "4"
```

As we could see there is a foreign key violation. That's because our lookup table `cities` doesn't have a city of `Munich` but we have a team from there. We need to fix it in `cities` resource:

```python
package.descriptor['resources'][1]['data'].append(['Munich', 'Germany'])
package.commit()
teams = package.get_resource('teams')
teams.check_relations()
# True
```

Fixed! But not only a check operation is available. We could use `relations` argument for `resource.iter/read` methods to dereference a resource relations:

```python
teams.read(keyed=True, relations=True)
#[{'id': 1, 'name': 'Arsenal', 'city': {'name': 'London', 'country': 'England}},
# {'id': 2, 'name': 'Real', 'city': {'name': 'Madrid', 'country': 'Spain}},
# {'id': 3, 'name': 'Bayern', 'city': {'name': 'Munich', 'country': 'Germany}}]
```

Instead of plain city name we've got a dictionary containing a city data. These `resource.iter/read` methods will fail with the same error as `resource.check_relations` does if there is an integrity issue.

issue_title: Allow two-step (i.e. retry-safe) task submission
issue_body:
`globus transfer async-transfer` and `globus transfer async-delete` currently hide away the submission ID generation. That means that the CLI doesn't support reliable, safe-to-retry task submission.

To resolve, we should expose this behavior with a new command and a couple of flags.

Namely, we want `globus transfer task generate-submission-id` to produce the submission ID in raw text, and `globus transfer async-transfer --submission-id` to consume it.
The same option should exist for `async-delete`, of course.

### What is wrong?

Currently, the `fix-unclean-shutdown` command is implemented directly in the main trinity code base. This could easily live under `trinity/plugins` similar to how the `trinity attach` command is handled.

Move code and wrap a plugin around it.

issue_title: Wagon ignores output directory
issue_body: When running wagon against a file system source project, it keeps trying to create the package in the current directory. For example, if the source project is in the current directory, it complains that the directory already exists. issue_title: conda config clear option
issue_body:
Would be great to have an option for `conda config` to `clear` a list entry of all items. For example, `conda config --clear channels` would change `condarc` like so.

```yaml
...
channels: []
...
```

issue_title: Document breaking changes in --json in command line, better python API doc
issue_body:
I'm working on updating conda from 4.2.13 to 4.3.8, I have been using 
```conda list --json```
to get the packages in a environment, that used to parse as a list of strings, but now it is a list of dicts, looks great! But would be nice if the change migrated to the changelog, (which I found at http://conda.pydata.org/docs/changelog.html). 

Side issue, better documentation on using the Python API for conda, the changelog mentions new things
```
conda.cli and conda.exports
```
I do the conda management from an environment other than root, so I need to know how to get those there, I once found a blog post about installing a conda xface package, https://github.com/conda/conda-api  but maybe if this made its way into the mainstream conda docs, would be helpful, and maybe that package is out of date?

Anyways, excited by all the new features in conda 4.3.8! Multi-user, etc. conda is getting more traction here, people like it.

issue_title: can't launch parallel shells (screen / tmux) while in virtualenv
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...**
  - [X] bug report
  - [ ] feature request

## Issue

Sorta related to #6800 and #2837, but this is still an issue in conda 4.4.11. If I am in a virtualenv X on linux, and then I run `screen` or `tmux` to create parallel shells, the spawned shells can claim to be in the same virtualenv X (i.e. it says "(X)" before the prompt) but actually aren't (based on which version of python opens and which packages are available). And there is no error message.

issue_title: conda should prefer the latest version from the request channel
issue_body:
Scenario:

hypothetical package call `pyconstants`.

`bcollins` channel has pyconstants 0.0.2 and 0.0.1
`dmertz` channel has pyconstants 0.0.1

`dmertz` installs `conda install -c bcollins pyconstants` and gets 0.0.2
`dmertz` decides he doesn't trust `bcollins` and runs `conda install -c dmertz pyconstants`

right now this says "pyconstants" already installed, instead of automatically using the latest version from `dmertz` which is `0.0.1`

issue_title: Install without tk
issue_body:
I want to be able to create a conda environment, specifically a python environment, without tk - that's a dependency I don't need (and is unnecessary when my code will eventually run on a headless server). However, if I try `conda remove tk` it also removes `pip, python, setuptools, wheel`. And if I then try to re-install, say, `pip`, it forces me to reinstall `tk` again. I don't need or want GUI support.

Any way around this?

Thanks

issue_title: "Detect and compile sub-modules" compiles additional sources even for nestes/multi programs
issue_body:
### Description

Sample

```cobol
       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. progA.
       DATA DIVISION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01  BIN PIC 9(9) BINARY VALUE 1.

       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
           CALL "subb" USING bin.
           CALL "subb" USING 4096.
       STOP RUN.

       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. subb.
       DATA DIVISION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

       LINKAGE SECTION.
       01  X  PIC 9(9) BINARY.

       PROCEDURE DIVISION USING X.
            DISPLAY ':' X ':'.
       END PROGRAM subb.
       END PROGRAM progA.

```

OCIDE tries to compile the program "subb" (just realized this as I had a broken source subb.cob laying around).

issue_title: Improve "field_stats" replacement with aggregation
msearch should be POST not GET
issue_body: The msearch request is sending GET as the method.  It should be POST as it is sending data in the post data.  Some proxies will strip out post data if the request is GET.

issue_title: ConnectionError caused by urllib3 upgrade to 1.17
issue_body:
Hello. Our `elasticsearch` dependency automatically pulled in the latest version of `urllib3` which is 1.17 (released 9/6/16). This broke our call to [Elasticsearch.search](https://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.io/en/master/api.html#elasticsearch.Elasticsearch.search) with this stack trace:

```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/elasticsearch/client/utils.py", line 69, in _wrapped
    return func(*args, params=params, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/elasticsearch/client/__init__.py", line 539, in search
    doc_type, '_search'), params=params, body=body)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/elasticsearch/transport.py", line 327, in perform_request
    status, headers, data = connection.perform_request(method, url, params, body, ignore=ignore, timeout=timeout)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/elasticsearch/connection/http_urllib3.py", line 105, in perform_request
    raise ConnectionError('N/A', str(e), e)
ConnectionError: ConnectionError(Codepoint U+002A at position 1 of u'*' not allowed) caused by: InvalidCodepoint(Codepoint U+002A at position 1 of u'*' not allowed)
```

The URL being connected to is the following (slightly modified for anonymity):

```
https://[REDACTED].us-east-1.aws.found.io:[REDACTED]/domains/domain/_search?search_type=scan&filter_path=_scroll_id%2Chits.hits._id%2Chits.hits._source.url%2Chits.hits._source.first_seen&scroll=1m&size=10000
```

My guess is that the certificate name is `*.us-east-1.aws.found.io` which would explain why there is an asterisk in the first position. The comment in [pyopenssl.py](https://github.com/shazow/urllib3/blob/master/urllib3/contrib/pyopenssl.py) explains why the DNS name gets encoded to idna in the first place:

```
Sadly the DNS names need to be idna encoded and then, on Python 3, UTF-8
decoded. This is pretty frustrating, but that's what the standard library
does with certificates, and so we need to attempt to do the same.
```

I've tracked down the [commit in urllib3](https://github.com/shazow/urllib3/commit/c5f393ae32b0caced3814a0011cd5f4bea3f63e9#diff-4d3aebf8115beaf5ef5f84a36e168b9c) which introduced this issue for us. Our mitigation was to add a direct dependency on `urllib3=1.16` which fixed the issue for the time being.

Is this a specific issue with Elasticsearch? I haven't been able to fully understand why this issue is happening, but I hoped opening an issue would help anyone else encountering this symptom. Is there a specific reason why there is no `urllib3` max version in your [setup.py](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/2.x/setup.py#L15)?

Thanks!

issue_title: VM for Marketplace
issue_body:
### Description
**Outline the issue here:**
I'm creating VM for Marketplace.
I prefer VM's cloud development, i.e. without on-premise HW.
According to https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/marketplace-publishing/marketplace-publishing-vm-image-creation in order to publish VM in the cloud I have to upload VHD image of my VM.

Some background information:

It looks like when our SSO settings were updated, there was a cert change that's not being picked up by my machine, and the new private cert on our login server isn't able to verify the signature on the SAML token signed by my machine.
